learnings.content: The Stripe API returns a 429 status code when you exceed 100 requests per second. Use exponential backoff with a base delay of 500ms and max 5 retries. The `Retry-After` header is present but not always accurate for burst limits.
learnings.context: Payment processing integration hitting rate limits during bulk invoice generation

learnings.content: GitHub Actions `actions/cache@v4` silently fails to restore cache when the key contains special characters like colons. Use dashes instead of colons in cache key names. The failure shows as a cache miss, not an error.
learnings.context: CI pipeline cache misses causing slow builds despite cache setup

learnings.content: PostgreSQL `VACUUM ANALYZE` should be run after bulk DELETE operations that remove more than 10% of rows. Without it, the query planner uses stale statistics and chooses sequential scans over index scans, causing 50-100x slower queries.
learnings.context: Slow query performance after data cleanup job

learnings.content: When deploying to AWS Lambda with Node.js 20, set `NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=1536'` to match the 2048MB memory allocation. The default 1536MB V8 heap is fine for 2GB Lambdas, but the 512MB default causes OOM on memory-intensive workloads.
learnings.context: Lambda function OOM crashes during PDF generation

learnings.content: The `better-sqlite3` package requires Python and a C++ compiler for native addon compilation. On Alpine Linux, install `build-base python3` before `npm install`. On macOS, Xcode Command Line Tools must be installed first.
learnings.context: Docker build failures with better-sqlite3 on Alpine base image

learnings.content: Redis `SCAN` with a MATCH pattern does NOT guarantee returning all matching keys in a single iteration. Always loop until the cursor returns 0. A COUNT hint of 100 is a good default -- too low causes many round trips, too high blocks the server.
learnings.context: Missing keys when listing cached sessions with SCAN

learnings.content: The `docker compose up --build` command does NOT invalidate build cache when only the `docker-compose.yml` file changes. Use `docker compose build --no-cache` explicitly when changing build args or environment variable definitions in the compose file.
learnings.context: Environment variable changes not taking effect after docker compose up --build

learnings.content: CORS preflight OPTIONS requests must return 204 (No Content), not 200. Some browsers (Safari 16+) reject 200 responses to preflight requests when the `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` header is missing. Always include all three CORS headers in OPTIONS responses.
learnings.context: Safari users unable to submit forms due to CORS errors

learnings.content: TypeScript `strict` mode enables `strictNullChecks`, `noImplicitAny`, `noImplicitThis`, `alwaysStrict`, `strictBindCallApply`, `strictPropertyInitialization`, and `strictFunctionTypes`. Enabling `strict` in an existing codebase adds 200-500 type errors per 10K lines. Migrate incrementally by enabling individual flags first.
learnings.context: Evaluating strict mode migration for legacy TypeScript project

learnings.content: The OpenAI `gpt-4o` model returns `finish_reason: 'length'` when output is truncated at `max_tokens`. Always check `finish_reason` and retry with higher `max_tokens` or implement streaming to detect truncation. The default `max_tokens` is 4096 but can be set up to 16384.
learnings.context: Incomplete JSON responses from GPT-4o during structured output generation

learnings.content: Kubernetes liveness probes should have `initialDelaySeconds: 30` for JVM applications and `initialDelaySeconds: 5` for Node.js. Setting it too low causes restart loops during startup. Readiness probes should have a shorter delay (5s for JVM, 2s for Node.js).
learnings.context: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff after deployment due to aggressive liveness probe

learnings.content: npm `--legacy-peer-deps` flag is required when installing packages in monorepos using npm v7+ with conflicting peer dependency trees. Without it, `npm install` fails with `ERESOLVE`. The flag is safe for development but should be investigated for production deployments.
learnings.context: npm install failing in monorepo after upgrading to npm 9

learnings.content: AWS S3 `PutObject` with server-side encryption (SSE-S3) requires the `x-amz-server-side-encryption: AES256` header on every upload request. Without it, objects are stored unencrypted even if the bucket policy requires encryption -- the policy only blocks reads of unencrypted objects, not writes.
learnings.context: Security audit finding that some S3 objects were not encrypted

learnings.content: The `Error: ENOSPC: no space left on device, write` error in Docker on Linux is usually caused by a full Docker overlay2 storage, not the host disk. Run `docker system prune -a --volumes` to reclaim space. Set `max-size` in Docker daemon.json log options to prevent log files from consuming all space.
learnings.context: Docker builds failing with ENOSPC despite 50GB free disk space

learnings.content: Cloudflare Workers have a 128MB memory limit and 30-second CPU time limit for paid plans (10ms for free). Subrequest limit is 50 per request (1000 for Enterprise). The `wrangler dev` local mode does NOT enforce these limits, so test in production with `wrangler dev --remote`.
learnings.context: Worker crashing in production but working in local development

learnings.content: SQLite `journal_mode=WAL` must be set before any concurrent reads. Setting it after opening the database in multi-threaded mode can cause `SQLITE_BUSY` errors. The PRAGMA returns the new mode as confirmation -- always check the return value.
learnings.context: Intermittent SQLITE_BUSY errors in production under concurrent load

learnings.content: The `fetch()` API in Node.js 18+ does NOT throw on HTTP error status codes (4xx, 5xx). You must check `response.ok` or `response.status` explicitly. This differs from Axios which throws on non-2xx responses by default.
learnings.context: Silent failures when API calls return 500 errors

learnings.content: Git `rebase --onto` is the correct command for moving a branch from one base to another. Syntax: `git rebase --onto new-base old-base branch-name`. Common mistake: using `git rebase new-base` which replays ALL commits, not just those after old-base.
learnings.context: Branch rebase accidentally replaying commits from wrong base

learnings.content: Supabase Edge Functions have a 150-second execution timeout and 2MB request body limit. The `Deno.serve` handler must return within the timeout or the response is dropped. For large payloads, use Supabase Storage upload with a signed URL instead of passing data through the function.
learnings.context: Edge function timeouts when processing large file uploads

learnings.content: The `zod` library v4 uses `z.string().email()` for email validation but the regex rejects valid addresses with `+` aliases (e.g., `user+tag@example.com`). Use a custom refinement or the `z.string().pipe(z.email())` pattern instead for production email validation.
learnings.context: User registration failing for users with + in their email address

learnings.content: The Gemini API `generativelanguage.googleapis.com` returns a `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` error (HTTP 429) when RPM quota is exceeded. The default free tier allows 60 RPM. Use the `Retry-After` header value (in seconds) for backoff timing. Batch embedding requests with up to 10 texts per call to stay under limits.
learnings.context: Embedding generation failing during bulk indexing

learnings.content: When using `vitest` with ESM modules, add `pool: 'forks'` to vitest.config.ts to avoid `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` errors with native addons like `better-sqlite3`. The default `threads` pool uses worker_threads which cannot load native ESM addons.
learnings.context: Vitest tests crashing with native addon import errors

learnings.content: Terraform `terraform plan` shows `~` (tilde) for in-place updates that won't cause downtime, and `- / +` for destroy-and-recreate changes. Always check for `- / +` on production resources before `terraform apply`. Use `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` on critical resources.
learnings.context: Terraform accidentally destroying and recreating a production database

learnings.content: NGINX returns `413 Request Entity Too Large` when the request body exceeds `client_max_body_size` (default 1MB). Set it in the `http`, `server`, or `location` block. For file uploads, also set `proxy_max_temp_file_size` if using NGINX as a reverse proxy.
learnings.context: File upload API returning 413 errors for files over 1MB

learnings.content: The `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` McpServer's `tool()` method accepts an optional 3rd argument for MCP annotations: `readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, `idempotentHint`, `openWorldHint`. These inform the client about tool behavior and help with confirmation prompts.
learnings.context: Adding proper MCP annotations to Strata tools for client safety

learnings.content: PostgreSQL `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` output shows actual execution time, but the `Buffers` information requires `SET track_io_timing = on` first. Without it, you can see row estimates vs actuals but not the I/O cost breakdown needed to diagnose slow queries caused by disk access.
learnings.context: Debugging slow queries but EXPLAIN ANALYZE not showing I/O timing

learnings.content: The `Error: listen EADDRINUSE :::3000` error means port 3000 is already in use. On Linux, use `lsof -ti:3000 | xargs kill -9` to find and kill the process. On macOS, use `lsof -i :3000`. On Windows, use `netstat -ano | findstr :3000` then `taskkill /PID <pid> /F`.
learnings.context: Development server failing to start because port is occupied

learnings.content: AWS CloudFront invalidation requests have a free tier of 1000 paths per month. After that, each invalidation costs $0.005 per path. Use wildcard invalidations (`/*`) to invalidate everything at once instead of listing individual paths. Invalidations take 5-10 minutes to propagate globally.
learnings.context: High CloudFront invalidation costs after implementing per-file cache busting

learnings.content: The `tsx` loader (TypeScript Execute) does NOT type-check at runtime -- it only transpiles. Use `tsc --noEmit` separately for type checking, or `vitest` which runs type checking alongside tests. Running `npx tsx src/index.ts` will happily execute code with type errors.
learnings.context: Type errors in production that passed tsx execution during development

learnings.content: Prisma `prisma generate` must be run after every `package.json` change that updates the prisma version, not just after schema changes. Forgetting this causes `PrismaClientInitializationError: This version of @prisma/client was compiled against schema X but the engine was generated for schema Y`.
learnings.context: Prisma client version mismatch after dependency update

learnings.content: Use `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'` checks for debug logging, not `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'`. In CI/CD and staging, NODE_ENV is often set to `test` or `staging`, which means development-only checks silently skip useful logging.
learnings.context: Missing debug logs in staging environment during incident investigation

learnings.content: The Slack Web API `chat.postMessage` has a 40,000 character limit per message. For longer content, use `files.upload` or split into multiple messages. The Block Kit `section` block has a separate 3000-character limit per text field.
learnings.context: Slack bot silently dropping messages that exceed character limit

learnings.content: When running `npm test` in CI with GitHub Actions, set `CI=true` environment variable. This makes Jest run in non-interactive mode, prevents watchman from starting, and causes `--watchAll` to be ignored. Without it, tests hang waiting for input.
learnings.context: CI pipeline hanging indefinitely during test step

learnings.content: The `crypto.randomUUID()` function is available in Node.js 16.7+ and all modern browsers. Do NOT use `uuid` npm package for basic UUID generation -- it adds 4KB to the bundle. For server-side Node.js, `crypto.randomUUID()` is 10x faster than the `uuid` package.
learnings.context: Bundle size optimization removing unnecessary uuid dependency

learnings.content: Vercel serverless functions have a 10-second timeout on the Hobby plan and 60 seconds on Pro. The response body limit is 4.5MB. For long-running tasks, use Vercel Cron Jobs or offload to a background queue. Edge Functions have a 25ms CPU time limit.
learnings.context: API route timeouts on Vercel Hobby plan during data processing

learnings.content: The `Error: ENAMETOOLONG` in Node.js occurs when a file path exceeds 255 characters (Linux) or 260 characters (Windows). This commonly happens with deeply nested `node_modules` paths. Use `npm dedupe` or switch to pnpm which uses a flat store with symlinks.
learnings.context: Windows developers unable to clone repo due to long file paths in node_modules

learnings.content: JSON.parse() in V8 is faster than JavaScript object literal evaluation for objects larger than 10KB. For static configuration objects, `JSON.parse('{...}')` is 2-3x faster than `const config = {...}`. This is because V8 can skip the AST parsing step for JSON strings.
learnings.context: Performance optimization for large static configuration objects

learnings.content: The `SIGTERM` signal in Docker gives containers 10 seconds to shut down gracefully before `SIGKILL`. Handle `SIGTERM` in your Node.js process to close database connections and flush buffers. Use `process.on('SIGTERM', () => { server.close(); db.close(); })` as the handler.
learnings.context: Database corruption due to ungraceful container shutdown

learnings.content: The `AbortController` timeout pattern for fetch requires creating a new controller per request. Reusing an already-aborted controller causes immediate abort of subsequent requests. Use `AbortSignal.timeout(5000)` in Node.js 18+ instead of manual controller management.
learnings.context: All HTTP requests failing after the first timeout when reusing AbortController

learnings.content: ESLint flat config (`eslint.config.js`) does NOT support `.eslintignore` files. Use the `ignores` array in the config object instead. The flat config also does not support `extends` -- use spread syntax to merge configs: `[...tseslint.configs.recommended, { rules: {} }]`.
learnings.context: ESLint v9 migration breaking ignore patterns and config extends

learnings.content: The `Error: EMFILE: too many open files` on macOS occurs when file descriptor limit is reached (default 256). Increase with `ulimit -n 10240` in the current shell. For permanent fix, create `/Library/LaunchDaemons/limit.maxfiles.plist` with soft limit 10240 and hard limit 10240.
learnings.context: Build tool crashing on large monorepo due to file descriptor exhaustion

learnings.content: Tailwind CSS `@apply` in component CSS files does NOT work with classes from plugins unless the plugin is loaded in the same PostCSS pipeline. For shadcn/ui components, ensure `tailwind.config.ts` includes the `content` path to the component library directory.
learnings.context: Tailwind utility classes not applying in shadcn/ui components

learnings.content: The `Bun.serve()` WebSocket handler requires `idleTimeout` to be set explicitly (default 120 seconds). Without it, idle connections stay open indefinitely, causing memory leaks. Set to 60 seconds for most applications. Use `ws.ping()` to keep connections alive.
learnings.context: Memory leak in Bun WebSocket server after running for 24 hours

learnings.content: When using `git worktree add`, the new worktree shares the same `.git` directory as the main repo. Running `git gc` from any worktree affects all worktrees. Prune worktrees with `git worktree prune` before running gc to avoid corruption warnings.
learnings.context: Git garbage collection warnings after removing a worktree directory manually

learnings.content: The `Error: ECONNRESET` during `npm install` usually indicates a corporate proxy or VPN intercepting HTTPS traffic. Set npm to use the system proxy with `npm config set proxy http://proxy:8080` and `npm config set https-proxy http://proxy:8080`. Also set `npm config set strict-ssl false` if the proxy uses a self-signed cert.
learnings.context: npm install failures behind corporate firewall

learnings.content: React `useEffect` cleanup functions run BEFORE the next effect, not on unmount only. This means a cleanup that cancels a fetch request will fire on every re-render with changed dependencies. Use an `AbortController` per effect instance, not a shared one.
learnings.context: Race condition in React component fetching data with useEffect

learnings.content: Cloudflare R2 has a 5GB maximum object size for single-part uploads. For larger files, use multipart upload with parts between 5MB and 5GB. The `@aws-sdk/client-s3` works with R2 -- set `endpoint` and `forcePathStyle: true` in the client config.
learnings.context: Large file upload failing silently to R2 bucket

learnings.content: The SQLite FTS5 `MATCH` query treats special characters as token separators. To search for an exact phrase containing special characters like hyphens, use double quotes: `documents_fts MATCH '"error-code"'`. Without quotes, `error-code` is split into two separate tokens.
learnings.context: FTS5 search not finding exact hyphenated terms

learnings.content: The `Error: Cannot find module` in ESM (import syntax) requires file extensions in import paths, unlike CommonJS. Use `.js` extension even for TypeScript files when `module: 'Node16'` is set. The TypeScript compiler resolves `.js` to `.ts` during compilation but the runtime needs `.js`.
learnings.context: ESM module resolution failing after migrating from CommonJS to ESM

learnings.content: Polar.sh webhook signatures use HMAC-SHA256 with the raw request body. Do NOT parse the body as JSON before verifying the signature -- JSON.parse + JSON.stringify changes whitespace and key ordering, which invalidates the signature. Use the raw body buffer.
learnings.context: Webhook signature verification failing after middleware parsed the body
